Identity.History: Chandragupta I ruled the Gupta Empire around 319–335 AD and is often regarded as its first great king. Through marriage to the Licchavi princess Kumaradevi, he gained prestige, alliances, and influence in northern India. He adopted the imperial title Maharajadhiraja, “king of great kings,” signaling Gupta ambitions beyond a regional kingdom. His reign likely consolidated Gupta power in the Ganges basin and laid the political foundation for later expansion under his son Samudragupta.
